title: synsepalum dulcificum
intro: Synsepalum dulcificum, also known as the miracle fruit, is a plant native to West Africa that contains a unique protein called miraculin. This protein has the ability to temporarily alter the taste buds on the tongue, making sour foods taste sweet. The medicinal properties of Synsepalum dulcificum include its potential use in treating hypertension and its antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infections. The health benefits of consuming the fruit include reduced sugar cravings and improved appetite, and herbal preparations can be made from the plant's leaves and seeds to create medicinal teas and infusions.
